---
name: obligatory_ai_generation_comment_compliance
description: Require exact AI generation marker comments in every newly created file and every newly created class. Always apply when creating new Java files, Python modules, test classes, configuration files, or any source file. The comment text must be exact — do not paraphrase.
---

# Obligatory AI Generation Comment Compliance

## Rule

For **every newly created file** and **every newly created class**, include the following comment text **exactly** as written:

```
generated by AI in Github cloud
```

## Placement rules

| Location | How to add the comment |
|----------|----------------------|
| Top of a new file | First or second line (after any mandatory shebang `#!` or package declaration) |
| New class definition | In a line comment directly above the `class` / `record` / `interface` / `enum` keyword **or** inside the class Javadoc block |

## Java examples

### New file header
```java
// generated by AI in Github cloud
import java.time.LocalDate;

// generated by AI in Github cloud
public class MyService {
    ...
}
```

### Inner class / record
```java
// generated by AI in Github cloud
record Point(int x, int y) {}
```

### Sealed interface
```java
// generated by AI in Github cloud
sealed interface Shape permits Shape.Circle, Shape.Rectangle {}
```

## Python example
```python
# generated by AI in Github cloud

class MyDataClass:
    ...
```

## Non-negotiable constraints
- The comment text must be **exact**: `generated by AI in Github cloud`
- Do **not** alter capitalisation, spacing, or wording
- Do **not** omit the comment for inner classes, records, sealed interfaces, or anonymous classes that are promoted to named types
- The comment is **not** required for modified (not newly created) files or classes, but if you add a **new** class to an existing file, add the comment above that new class
